title: Eva 20 You Can Not Advance screening in Italy Update
display_title: Eva 20 You Can Not Advance screening in Italy Update
wikitext:
[]: Eva 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance screening in Italy [Update]             Quote Post by Tetsuo87 » Tue Oct 20, 2009 5:36 pm Evangelion 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance screening in Italy I am really excited... Italian distributor "Dynit" announced this evening that the fair "Lucca Comics and Games" which is precisely in Lucca from 29 October to 1 November will be screened the films Evangelion 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance , in Japanese with English subtitles . Eva 2.0 was also screened at the "Romics" who was in Rome last September 29. The screening will take place only Sunday, November 1st, at 14.00 (GMT +1), at the Teatro San Girolamo in Lucca. free tickets are available at the stand Dynit present in the fair. Tickets are free but places are limited! official statement (from the site Dynit automatically translated): Full calendar of screenings and meetings this year at Lucca Comics & Games 2009! In particular we are waiting for Sunday, November 1st preview of Evangelion 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance!. Warning! Free screenings by invitation. For tickets contact booth Dynit (limited places) In addition to Dynit conference scheduled for Saturday 31/10, during which reveals what the 2010, is expected to host Carlo Freccero (Director RAI4) After the success of Code Geass and Gurren Lagann! Dynit awaits this year to stand in Hall Publishers in P.zza Napolene with all the news out! Teatro San Girolamo THURSDAY '29/10 12:30 hours Dynit presents: Lovely Complex (25 ', p .1, Version in Italian) FRIDAY '30/10 12:00 Dynit presents: Astrop (90 ', the Icelandic language version with Italian subtitles) Saturday, 31/10 - 13:15 Dynit presents: Series surprise (25 ') - Ore 13:40 Dynit meets the audience participates Carlo Cavazzoni (Executive Director Dynit) - 14:30 RAI4 meets the audience participates Carlo Freccero (Director RAI4) - Ore 15:10 And present RAI4 Dynit: Code Geass (25 ', p .7, Version in Italian) RAI4 and Dynit present: Gurren Lagann (25 ', p .7, Version in Italian) Sunday 1 / 11 - Ore 14:00 Dynit presents: Evangelion 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance (90 ', Japanese version with English subtitles)

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Tue Oct 20, 2009 5:45 pm Lucky bastard. /jk/ YO! KHARA AND FUNI! GET YOR BUTTS IN GEAR AND BRING IT TO THE BLOODY STATES ALREADY!

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Tue Oct 20, 2009 6:09 pm Wha- 2.0 was actually screened at Romics?!? Thank you for the info - seriously considering a trip to Lucca now! \0/

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Tue Oct 20, 2009 6:11 pm WHAT? WHAT? WHAT? I have some friends that go to Lucca Comics every year, I might find a way to get there with them. They know NGE but aren't really fans of the show, maybe Rebuild can change their mind... I'll have to find a good excuse to explain the trip to my parents though, as you know, someone my age can't possibly be interested in stupid Japanese cartoons ;) EDIT: damn, it's the same day as the Abu Dhabi Grand Prix. Oh well.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Oct 20, 2009 6:11 pm Post made. http://www.evageeks.org/2009/10/evangelion-2-0-screening-in-italy/ Edit: Fixed an error in the post. Lucky indeed. Be sure to watch and give your thoughts!

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Oct 20, 2009 6:14 pm LiLi wrote: Wha- 2.0 was actually screened at Romics?!? Thank you for the info - seriously considering a trip to Lucca now! \0/

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Tue Oct 20, 2009 6:17 pm ath wrote: I have some friends that go to Lucca Comics every year, I might find a way to get there with them.

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Tue Oct 20, 2009 6:35 pm Curse you Italy! Get off your asses Funi!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Oct 20, 2009 6:40 pm I mentioned this in some other...thread (forget which) but according to the 1.0 Dub Next Time Preview for 2.0, Misato promises it'll be "coming soon" whatever that would entail.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Tue Oct 20, 2009 6:43 pm So in other words, about a year or two till its release in America right?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Oct 20, 2009 6:44 pm schismatics wrote: Curse you Italy! Get off your asses Funi!

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Tue Oct 20, 2009 6:53 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: schismatics wrote: Curse you Italy! Get off your asses Funi!

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Oct 21, 2009 4:06 am Damn you, you lucky Italianos! If only... if only...*thinks about any of the Rebuild films screening in Hungary*

[]:             Quote Post by Shooting Star » Wed Oct 21, 2009 8:15 am Italia? and we? in France we eva 2.0 version under the original French title October 28 in Nantes ( Utopiales, le festival international de science-fiction de Nantes, ) and November 8 in Lyon ( Lyon Asian Film Festival ) we know, we know... in USA you are (not) lucky

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Oct 21, 2009 8:32 am you goddamn french fries and pizzas We tried to get that film to R&A this year but didn't get it. *sigh* Yet again Finland is USA of Europe!

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Wed Oct 21, 2009 1:48 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: schismatics wrote: Curse you Italy! Get off your asses Funi!

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Oct 21, 2009 2:10 pm master_lloyd wrote: Ze Germans haff vays of procuring zeir anime qvuickly.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Wed Oct 21, 2009 2:26 pm Well, Italy (and maybe France?) has been importing anime (aka 'Japanese cartoons') rather massively for over 30 years now... that might be a factor in the equation. By the way people here talk about anime, I get the impression it's more "niche" in the USA? I also get the impression that among the most popular titles, the anime movies the release of which got slowed down the most in my country are probably those Buena Vista retained the rights for...

[]:             Quote Post by Lui » Wed Oct 21, 2009 3:42 pm Well it's certainly not going to be screened in the UK, which sucks, as I live in the UK and the only way I'll be able to watch it is to wait for the DVD to be available for preorder. Anime isn't really popular over here... Only stuff like Naruto and Pokemans etc.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Wed Oct 21, 2009 4:04 pm LiLi wrote: But what if I get there and there are no tickets left?
